title: aruncus dioicus subsp. triternatus
intro: Aruncus dioicus subsp. triternatus, also known as the mountain goat's beard, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) and is native to the western United States. The plant's roots and leaves are used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and are often used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Aruncus dioicus subsp. triternatus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make topical applications for skin conditions or taken internally to support overall health and well-being.
